newsSinceJun6: No material fundamental news. CEO Scott Keeney sold ~25,000 shares (~$1.9M) on June 3-4 (mostly 10b5-1 plan plus RSU tax withholding), disclosed around June 6. nLIGHT announced participation at Eurosatory 2026 (June 15-19, Paris) showcasing the HADES 70 kW directed-energy laser module - a defense catalyst, not an AI/CPO one. Today's -6.6% extends a ~11% pullback from May highs that commentators attribute to stretched valuation after a ~353% one-year run, not to any thesis-breaking event; the move looks flow/valuation-driven.

sources.label: Motley Fool - nLIGHT CEO sold ~25,000 shares for $1.9M (Jun 6, 2026)
StockTitan - nLIGHT to showcase 70 kW HADES laser at Eurosatory 2026

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already demoted LASR for post-rerating crowding; today's -6.6% flush (now ~11% off May highs) came on no fundamental negative, so the asymmetry at rank 10 is modestly restored rather than further impaired. No new AI/CPO evidence either way - the only fresh items (CEO 10b5-1 sales, Eurosatory defense showcase) sit outside this page's photonics-bottleneck lens.
verdicts.bull: Vertically integrated semiconductor-laser platform with optionality into AI photonics/CPO, and the -6.6% valuation flush improves entry without any thesis damage; defense directed-energy revenue (+69% A&D in Q1) funds the photonics franchise.
verdicts.neutral: The 62.5% YTD run was driven by defense directed-energy, not CPO design wins, so residual alpha on this AI/CPO page is thinner than the score implies; the pullback merely offsets prior crowding.
verdicts.bear: Still richly valued after a ~353% one-year run with CEO insider selling on the tape; if the defense narrative cools and no CPO/AI laser wins materialize, the stock de-rates further toward bear-case fair values near $53.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Eurosatory 2026 (June 15-19) HADES 70 kW showcase and any follow-on directed-energy contract awards; any disclosed AI/datacenter photonics design win would re-validate this page's thesis.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Valuation compression continuing - the rally was defense-narrative-driven and largely orthogonal to the CPO bottleneck thesis, leaving the position exposed to multiple contraction without page-relevant offsetting news.
